### System Prompt:

Analyze the given Inputs and observe the Name Entity Recognition <NER> entity pairs in <CANDIDATE_PAIRS> to classify the entity pair into one of the relations in the <NER-NER Relation List>
Note: 
- <CANDIDATE_PAIRS> are derived from chunked data, meaning identical text may appear with different [E#] IDs. You must treat each unique [E#] ID as a distinct, independent entity.
- Be mutually exclusive yet completely exhaustive. 
- Be aware of the directionality of relations. Based on subject/object order. Inverse relations can be established as well. (Ex. "used", "used_by")
- The "relation" key can't be null. If there is no relationship return "NO_RELATION"<turn|>
<|turn>user
### Output Format:
The output format MUST be in JSON. Fill in a relation for every <CANDIDATE_PAIR>, if none exists return "NO_RELATION". Every set should consist of these 4 required keys in this specific order: pair_id, subject, relation, and object. Remember the pair_id, subject, and object MUST be exactly from the <CANDIDATE_PAIR> and the relation MUST be from the <Relation List>.
